key_insight: Cepstral analysis makes Green-Kubo thermal conductivity calculations for MOFs far more stable, faster to converge, and less dependent on user-chosen parameters.
engineering_relevance: This enables more reliable, automation-ready prediction of heat transport in porous materials, supporting thermal management and device design for MOF-based applications.
method: Use equilibrium molecular dynamics with Green-Kubo heat-flux autocorrelation.
Apply cepstral analysis to suppress statistical noise and reduce sensitivity to correlation-length choices.
Train machine-learned moment tensor potentials on DFT reference data for near-ab initio accuracy.
evidence_signal: The approach was demonstrated on MOF-5, HKUST-1, and ZIF-8, where it converged within about 1–2 ns and outperformed direct Green-Kubo analysis in stability across correlation lengths.
watch_item: Results depend on the quality and transferability of the machine-learned potential, so the workflow still requires careful validation against reference data.
paper_title: Cepstral Analysis to accelerate Green-Kubo thermal conductivity calculations of Metal-Organic Frameworks
